feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Adjusting an existing Datasphere connection without affecting imported remote tables
entry.content:
Hi,

Could you help me with this question? We have an established connection via Generic JDBC between Datasphere and CRM that uses a PostgreSQL driver (org.postgresql.Driver with URL jdbc:postgresql:). The Postgres database was recently updated to a new version, and I adjusted that same connection. It's the same port, same tables, so I only adjusted the IP address and the new username and password. However, the existing remote tables, which were already imported from that connection, stopped replicating.

I tried the following:

- Creating a new test connection and importing a test table, and it replicates without problems.

- From the existing (and adjusted) connection, I imported another table, and it replicates without problems.

- I also tried stopping automatic replication, changing the source, and reconnecting to the same connection, and updating replication, but without success.

Apparently, the error is only... For existing remote tables originating from that configured source, the error message displayed is as follows:

Error: {"timestamp":"2026-04-21 11:10:30.7592660", "origin":"_sys_task", "message":"internal error: DPAdapterResultSet::Fetch:Camel execution failed. at ptime/query/plan_executor/dml/hex/qe_hex_search.cc:512; Statement: ,INSERT INTO \"XXXXX\".\"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_CT\" (\"idprevisaovendas\", \"idparceiro\", \"idsafra\", \"idtabeladepreco\", \"idusuario\", \"idlocal\", \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"datacadastro\", \"idfilial\", \"integrado\", \"idcotacaoptax\", \"valorcotacaoptax\", \"idpvmoeda\", \"idpvtabelapreco\", \"valorcotacao\", \"chaveerp\", \"ano\") SELECT \"idprevisaovendas\", \"idparceiro\", \"idsafra\", \"idtabeladepreco\", \"idusuario\", \"idlocal\", \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"datacadastro\", \"idfilial\", \"integrado\", \"idcotacaoptax\", \"valorcotacaoptax\", \"idpvmoeda\", \"idpvtabelapreco\", \"valorcotacao\", \"chaveerp\", \"ano\" FROM (WITH _SYS_WITH_1 AS (SELECT \"idprevisaovendas\" , \"idparceiro\" , \"idsafra\" , \"idtabeladepreco\" , \"idusuario\" , \"idlocal\" , \"idgrupoempresarial\" , \"datacadastro\" , \"idfilial\" , \"integrado\" , \"idcotacaoptax\" , \"valorcotacaoptax\" , \"idpvmoeda\" , \"idpvtabelapreco\" , \"valorcotacao\" , \"chaveerp\" , \"ano\" FROM \"XXXXX\".\"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT\" AS \"XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT\") , _SYS_WITH_0 AS (SELECT \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idprevisaovendas\" AS \"idprevisaovendas\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idparceiro\" AS \"idparceiro\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idsafra\" AS \"idsafra\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idtabeladepreco\" AS \"idtabeladepreco\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idusuario\" AS \"idusuario\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idlocal\" AS \"idlocal\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idgrupoempresarial\" AS \"idgrupoempresarial\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"datacadastro\" AS \"datacadastro\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"idfilial\" AS \"idfilial\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public.previsaovendas_VT_SRC\".\"integrado\" AS \"integrado\", \"XXXXX_XXXXX_CRM_XXXXX::public."} 

The goal is to avoid creating a new connection, as that would require re-importing all the remote tables. I already have views and models created using those tables, and I'm unsure how this would impact things. Is it possible to avoid creating a new connection?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards!

entry.title: Deleting records from replicated table
entry.content:
Hi

deleting data from a local table seems straight forward but can we delete data from a replicated table?

Scenario:
The source system is itself a replica of another using change data capture. Each morning it is purged (using a truncate) and repopulated with data that is only relevant to the current day (ie ignores anything in the source that was completed before the current day). This accumulates during the day.
So in Datasphere we have set up real-time replication.
It works well for newly inserted records, updates or deleted records that occur throughout the day.

The problem is that it does not delete all data that is purged overnight, no doubt because it doesn't capture the deletions that occur as a result of the truncate (rightly or wrongly, that's another debate). This means that DS holds more data than is necessary, and the volume increases rapidly over a few days

So what I would like to do is selectively delete any records from DS that would have been purged.

I know one alternative solution would be to change the CDC in the source from truncate to delete *, but that's not currently an option.

entry.title: SAP BDC: Share table from disk storage to HANA lake file spaces
entry.content:
Hello All, 

 I am trying to load a flat file in SAP HANA Data Lake Files but as i read the blogs, its not possible. Its possible only disk for storage spaces. 

I want to basically upload this flat file , transform it ( as a POC, we want to do it in Datasphere) and share it with Databricks ( Delta share, zero copy) , perform AI activities and load the data back into datapshere. 

Can someone guide me step by step process and are there any limitation to request.

entry.title: Datashpere Migration to S4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I need your guidance regarding the SAP Datasphere migration to S/4HANA.

Previously, my datasphere was connected to ECC. Now, I want to migrate the  to S/4HANA. I have used multiple graphical views and created reporting-layer objects, which are also consumed in SAC.

In ECC, I was using remote tables and integrating them with local tables through data flows. However, in S/4HANA, all master data structures have changed (such as GL, Cost Center, Profit Center, Company Code, Buis area etc.).

Could you please suggest the best approach to handle these changes in the reporting layer?

Where exactly changes required?

Remote table also available in S4HANA.

Note: I need to retain ECC (PY) historical data along with current data from S/4HANA.

@jeet_kapase @cardoso  @N1kh1l 

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: Assistance Required – Date Format Handling in Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We need your assistance for the following query:

We are loading data from S/4HANA to Datasphere, where date-related fields are initially received in string format (e.g., 20251231 in YYYYMMDD format). We are converting these using the TO_DATE() function, where Date field ID results in the format 2025-12-31 (YYYY-MM-DD) and its behaving as per Datasphere default format.

Here, business expectation on Date ID format is 31.12.2025 (DD.MM.YYYY) and as per our knowledge we can change only the description by user-profile date format settings but not the Key-Value (2025-12-31 (YYYY-MM-DD)).

So, here business is expecting is there any option to change the Date key value format too into 31.12.2025 (DD.MM.YYYY) ?

Could you please review this and advise on possible solutions?

Regards,
Radhakrushna

entry.title: How Actual and planning data cab be reported combinedly in SAC?
entry.content:
HI Team,

Could you please check and share how we can achieve below scenario in SAP Data Sphere and SAC?

Lets say, I have Sales data in data sphere tables which is actuals and wanted to load this data to SAC for planning. After planning, I want to report actuals and plan in same report in SAC. 

1. Can we store actuals also in SAC along with planning? Is it a good approach?

2. Should I access actual data from data sphere to SAC using live connection and prepare the plan data in SAC. In this case, is plan data stores in SAC or shall I push the plan data also to Data sphere to do the combine reporting from Data sphere to SAC using live connection.

3. Or do we need to maintain the actuals in Data sphere and plan data in SAC, and do the reporting using some views or models in SAC.

Which is the best approach to do the actuals and planning data in same report in SAC. Kindly share your feedback.

entry.title: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphere Live model?
entry.content:
Hi experts, I would like to know when using the Datasphere live model in SAC, how can I achieve the SAC native Account dimension reporting capabilities, like aggregations and calculations on the hierarchy nodes? If my requirement is to develop a financial KPI based report on Datasphere Live model with calculated nodes in the account hierarchy structure how to do that?

Example:  Calculating the operating income KPI on a calculation from Revenue - Operating expenses, keep the operating income in the GL account hierarchy structure. If I create a measure based calculation, there will be more measures, but Finance team would like to see that in a account hierarchy.

entry.title: Sapces in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi All,

Regarding Spaces in Datasphere

1.Can we have same object in two spaces ?

2.Normally we can share the objects Table or View from one space to another space? My question is , if we share Object Table-A  from Space-1 to Space-2 , do we have Object Table-A in both the spaces (Space-1 & Space-2) or only in Sapce-2.

Please guide me if this form is not the correct forum for posting the questions related to SAP Datasphere.

Regards / SJ

entry.title: SAP Datasphere & BDC - Is system readiness analysis required in Phase0(Discovery)?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I’m working on a BTP-led data modernization program with the following target architecture:

SAP Datasphere (core data layer)
SAP HANA Cloud (persistence)
SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) for data products/orchestration
SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) for reporting
Our current approach is:

Phase 0 (Discovery):

Report rationalization (BO/Webi)
System analysis (ECC/HANA/reporting landscape)
System readiness / baseline assessment (data volume, extractors, custom logic, dependencies)
Phase 1 (Implementation):

BDC platform setup
S/4HANA readiness recommendations
BPC to SAC migration
We are also aligning with SAP’s BDC modernization assessment approach:
https://www.sap.com/registration/sap-bdc-modernization-assessment.html

Question
From an SAP recommended approach:

-->Is completing system readiness / source system analysis in Phase 0 considered a best practice prerequisite before implementation decisions (Datasphere/BDC setup)?

-->Or is it acceptable to defer this analysis to Phase 1?

Context
The goal is to ensure Phase 0 decisions (architecture, migration strategy, platform setup) are based on actual system insights such as:

Data volume and growth
BW extractors / SLT dependencies
Custom transformations
Reporting complexity

Appreciate any guidance based on SAP best practices or project experience.

Thanks,
Vishala.

entry.title: SAC-Datasphere Location Dimension Issue at Choroplet map
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I faced issue in our TDD system with Location Dimension. I created Dimension view and created calculated column as Geo-Dimension with latitude and longitude in Datasphere. I did all process to use this dimension in SAP Analytics Cloud. Location dimension is working fine for Bubble and Heat Map but when i tried to see at Choroplet map feauture i get error message as below: 

i worked with same dataset in other system with same process and it works fine for all map features but in our SAC system it's not working. What can be the issue?

entry.title: Adding columns to an active Replication Flow (S/4HANA to S3) without losing CDC Delta pointer
entry.content:
Hi all,

We currently have a Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere moving data from S/4HANA to an Amazon S3 bucket using the "Initial and Delta" load type.

The source is a custom CDS View with CDC annotations that joins two SAP tables (EKKO/EKPO) and currently has 84 columns.

The question is: If we need to add new columns to this CDS View in the future, is there a way to incorporate them into the existing Replication Flow without triggering a full Re-initialization (Full Load)?

We want to avoid losing the current Delta pointer (subscription) in ODP/ODQ, as a full reload of these tables would be highly time-consuming due to the volume of data. Is there any "Schema Evolution" support or a best practice to handle this "on-the-fly"?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Delta enabled replication Options in SAP Datasphere with the focus on using SAP extractors 2LIS
entry.content:
Hi,

Could someone please help me with an issue? I am extracting data from an S/4 system using 2LIS extractors. I have completed all prerequisites, including enabling the primary keys. However, when I try to run the delta-enabled replication, I am getting an error.

Could anyone please guide me on how to resolve this and successfully achieve the required data load?

please find the error attached

entry.title: Customer influence - please vote for ECC on HANA connector for Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

for our SAP ECC environment (ECC on HANA with S4 Finance 1605 activated), 70% of our extraction to the current BW platform is done with abap CDS views via ODP. This is currently not possible in Datasphere/BDC. As a result, we cannot develop most of our usecases on BDC to take advantage of the databricks delta share.

We want to innovate in BDC/Databricks, but also on data from "older" systems.

If you have similar issues, please vote for https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/373140.

If we have enough votes, SAP might want to consider fixing this.

Thanks,

Christophe Posson

entry.title: SAC and Datasphere to use same Idp for SSO
entry.content:
Hi,

We are looking into setting up Seamless Planning and also reporting on a tunnel connection in SAC with data from Datasphere.

Can see that we will have to use the same IdP to make it happen, so how do we get the possibility in Datasphere to make the same IdP as in SAC.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3720272

 

Best regards

Jesper Bonde

entry.title: Cloud Connector Setup for Datasphere Connection
entry.content:
I am currently configuring the connectivity between SAP Cloud Connector and an SAP Datasphere tenant.
The subaccount has already been successfully added to the Cloud Connector, and the on‑premise systems that need to be accessed have also been configured.

However, I am unclear about which specific resources must be defined and exposed for each on‑premise system (for example: paths, protocols, ports, and/or required resources) in order for SAP Datasphere to access them correctly through the Cloud Connector.

I would appreciate your support in validating which resources are required depending on the type of on‑premise system, and in confirming whether there are any recommended configurations or official documentation for this scenario.

entry.title: Missing Association After Data Product Deployment in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

A Sales table is modeled as a fact view with an association to a Product dimension (modeled as a separate graphical view). Both views are exposed for consumption. A data product is created on top of these two artifacts - Sales and Product and deployed. And the data product is installed in the target space while both views are available in the target space, the association is missing. Is this by design.

Could you help clarify why this happens. Thanks!
--
KRPK

entry.title: Replication flow and SAP conversion routine
entry.content:
Hello all

I'm facing with a little problem and a I would like see if you guys already know it and maybe any kind of solution.

The problem:

I have replication flow configured getting data from SAP-ECC system. It's been working fine but for some tables that have data element with conversion routine (Eg: T001 LANGU) Datasphere only consider external format I mean in DB table we have LANG as E (English) but external format is (EN) . 

I have some scenarios that must be just one character (w/o conversion routine) such as in DB.

Does any one had the same problem ?

Although i have an workaround in track but I'm trying look for options (I mean more clear solutions).

Thanks in Advance

entry.title: How to replicate FAGLFLEXT from SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
We are currently migrating our SAP ECC to S4 HANA.

We used to replicate data to SAP DSP via SAP SLT. We know many tables are changing so we are leveraging the ingestion of CDS views to fix some replication issues.

However, we are struggling to ingest FAGLFLEXT into SAP DSP. The CDS view created on top of compatibility view has all the OBJNR00~08 key field set to 0 (for the reason detailed here: 3380889 - Object number fields OBJNR00~08 is 0 in FAGLFLEXT after upgrade to S/4 HANA - SAP for Me). So, when we load te data from the CDS into SAP DSP, the system is working as a SELECT DISTINCT on the key because out of millions of records displayed in the source system, we replicate only few records (the ones with the Primary Key distinct).

We are using the FAGLFLEXT downstream so I'd like to understand which are the suggestion to keep this ingestion running as expected.

Thanks and regards,

i

entry.title: Best solutions/suggestions to bring SAP Datasphere data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle system
entry.content:
Dear SAP Colleagues & Experts

We are looking for best approach/suggestion to bring SAP Datasphere data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle system.

  1. DB connect can help to bring the data from Datasphere (Source) to SAP BW (Target)? If possible, in that case what are the per-requisite
  2. SAP Datasphere can place data to SAP BW 7.5 on Oracle file server path, further to create a datamodel 

Best regards,

Venkata

entry.title: How to transfer Object Ownership from One User to Another User in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
HI All,

How to transfer Object Ownership from One User to Another User in SAP Datasphere.

Couple of associates moved out of the project ,hence we would liek to transfer those objects which are created or changed by them to new users in SAP Datasphere.

1.DO we need to transfer object ownership from one user to another user.If yes, then can you please share me the step by step process.( I checked but do not find any option for Object Transfer)

2.If we do not transfer and de-activate or delete the user then what happened? Will objects will not work ?

3.If any objects which are scheduled by those users , hope those flows ( Background jobs/flows) will not schedule.

Can you please advise, thankyou
